published_prior_art.cite: Pearl1995CausalDiagrams: front-door functional identifies the ACE at the no-direct-path/exclusion corner

repo_prior_art.cite: none located in doc/API.md or formalization catalogue

published_prior_art.cite: standard Weierstrass compactness plus nested feasible-set endpoint monotonicity; Manski1990NonparametricBounds is background for bounded-outcome partial ID, not this leak-indexed model

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_proximal_l2_bridge concerns proximal bridge L2 endpoint geometry and inference; no single-clean-mediator leaky-front-door ACE set appears in the catalogue

published_prior_art.cite: Pearl1995CausalDiagrams gives only the lambda=0 front-door point; FulcherShpitserMarealleTchetgen2017GeneralizedFrontDoor targets PIIE; DingVanderWeele2016SharpMediationSensitivity treats mediator-outcome confounding; FrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2023SharpBounds gives a generic sensitivity family, not the clean-mediator shared-U tightening

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovHongTamer2007ParameterSets, FangSantos2019DirectionallyDifferentiable, and GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023ProxyPartialID supply general set-inference/directional-delta/proxy-bound templates, but not this endpoint map

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 3 and Open-ended Questions 4-5
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Strict inclusion, finite-vertex endpoint construction, and endpoint inference are intentional open kernels, not proved results at proposal stage.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: field-tier proposal conditional on resolving the open kernels; Theorems 1-2 are support results only

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 / endpoint reporting
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Connectedness of I(lambda) is not proved; the proposal establishes compactness and endpoint monotonicity and should not claim every value in [L(lambda),U(lambda)] is attainable until the OEQ4 route resolves it.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: treat the current headline as sharp endpoint/convex-hull reporting unless connectedness is later proved

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 4
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The vertex/kink construction is scoped to the locked finite-support convention K<=5 with lambda*=+infty under the no-kink convention.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: no claim beyond the finite-support endpoint construction and its stated no-kink regime

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add one sentence in the abstract or §8 clarifying endpoint interval versus full connected identified set, matching the §11 caveat, so Stage 0 does not overclaim sharp connectedness.
In §9, extend the rational kappa(P)=1/4 witness with at least one computed feasible value from I(lambda) and one relaxed value from B_box(lambda) once the active-cell enumeration starts.

Add the 2026 Wu-Robeva generalized front-door preprint as a related exact-ID comparator if the bibliography is refreshed; it appears orthogonal to partial ID but is now the closest recent front-door generalization.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 on this angle; novelty and well-posedness clear at field tier with accepted endpoint-scope caveats.
